actions.rationale: COHR remains above the 30% soft cap at 38.41%, but this is a passive overweight driven by prior price action, not a reason to force-trim per Serenity rules. Q3 FY2026 EPS $1.41 (est $1.43) and revenue $1.8B (est $1.815B) were slight misses, but the AI datacenter optics thesis remains intact. The SemiAnalysis CPO-delay report triggered an optical-sector selloff (COHR down ~11%), but industry rebuttals argue CPO delays affect form factors, not the shared chokepoint layers (lasers, components) that COHR controls. No cash available to add, and soft-cap rules prohibit increasing an already-overweight position. Hold and let thesis play out.

LITE reported a blowout Q3 FY2026: revenue $808.4M (+91% YoY), EPS $2.37, with strong Q4 guidance of $960M-$1.01B revenue and $2.85-$3.05 EPS. The SemiAnalysis report dragged LITE down ~8%, creating a buying-opportunity setup in a thesis-intact name. However, with only $353.26 cash, there is insufficient capital to add even a single share ($889.59). Hold existing position and preserve dry powder for a future add if cash becomes available.

MRVL is the CPO/DCP exposure in the portfolio and the only position with a gain (+3.90%). S&P 500 inclusion (June 2026) is a positive near-term catalyst. Recent product announcements (102.4 Tbps AI switch, 260-lane PCIe 6.0 switch) reinforce its position in the AI datacenter connectivity stack. Adding even one share would push MRVL to ~32.6%, breaching the 30% soft cap. With no compelling reason to trim and no room to add under the cap, hold.

summary: No trades today. All three positions (COHR, LITE, MRVL) are HOLD. The SemiAnalysis CPO-delay report triggered a sector-wide optical selloff, but industry pushback argues the delay affects packaging form factors, not the scarce laser/component layers that COHR and LITE dominate. LITE's Q3 beat (+91% revenue growth) and raised Q4 guidance confirm the optical demand thesis is intact. MRVL continues to benefit from S&P 500 inclusion and new AI switch products. COHR sits above the 30% soft cap (38.41%), but Serenity's framework explicitly does not force-trim passive overweights. Cash is only $353.26 (3.73%), insufficient to add any position without breaching soft caps. Best action is to hold core positions and preserve optionality.
strongest_bear_case: SemiAnalysis may be directionally correct that CPO mass production slips to 2028-2029. While the rebuttal argues this only affects form-factor timing and not the shared bottleneck layers (lasers, DSPs), the market has already shown it will trade optical names as a 'CPO basket.' If institutional investors rotate out of optical ahead of a multi-year wait for CPO revenue, the sector could face sustained multiple compression even as near-term transceiver demand stays strong. Additionally, COHR's slight Q3 miss ($1.41 vs $1.43 EPS) could signal margin pressure or mix-shift headwinds that worsen if competition intensifies.
